steps.title: Open the raw lipidomics data in Microsoft Excel
steps.instruction: Open the raw lipidomics data in Microsoft Excel.

steps.title: Replace missing values in the raw...
steps.instruction: Replace missing values in the raw data with 0 to avoid calculation errors.

steps.title: Average the replicates of the MOCK control:
steps.instruction: Average the replicates of the MOCK control:

steps.title: Group lipids into categories
steps.instruction: Group lipids into categories: Cholesterol PC : Includes PC and LPC PE : Includes PE and LPE PI PS SM OTHERS : Includes CE, Cer, DAG, GlcCer, LacCer, PG, and TAG

steps.title: Generate summary tables or graphs for...
steps.instruction: Generate summary tables or graphs for visualization and statistical analysis as needed.

steps.title: For each lipid species, calculate the...
steps.instruction: For each lipid species, calculate the mean value across the three biological replicates.

steps.title: Subtract the averaged MOCK control values...
steps.instruction: Subtract the averaged MOCK control values from each replicate of the ATG9A vesicle samples for each lipid species.

steps.title: If the result is negative, set the value to 0
steps.instruction: If the result is negative, set the value to 0. The background-corrected values were then used to calculate the final %mol values.

steps.title: Use the formula
steps.instruction: Use the formula: %mol = (individual lipid species / total lipids of that condition) × 100

steps.title: For each condition (after background subtraction),...
steps.instruction: For each condition (after background subtraction), sum all lipid species to obtain the total lipid content.

steps.title: To obtain the final lipid composition,...
steps.instruction: To obtain the final lipid composition, calculate the mean molar percentage across all replicates (for each group of lipids).
